description: Bulk triage and fix GitHub issues in parallel using isolated worktrees
author: claude-sonnet-4-6
use_cases: 10+ open GitHub issues that are simple one-file fixes
Auto-generated follow-up issues with many duplicates
Independent fixes requiring no cross-issue coordination
prerequisites: CI/CD pipeline configured with auto-merge support

workflow_summary.step_0: Triage all issues — close duplicates and already-fixed items first
workflow_summary.step_1: Analyze file overlap across remaining issues to detect conflicts
workflow_summary.step_2: Group issues into waves by complexity and risk level
workflow_summary.step_3: Launch up to 5 parallel agents per wave using isolation: worktree
workflow_summary.step_4: Each agent runs targeted pre-commit, tests, then creates PR with auto-merge

key_commands.close_duplicate: gh issue comment <n> --body 'Duplicate of #X' && gh issue close <n>
key_commands.run_unit_tests: pixi run python -m pytest tests/unit/ -q --no-cov 2>&1 | tail -20

lessons_learned: Never run pre-commit --all-files in agents — use targeted hooks only to avoid contamination
Pre-analyze file overlap before execution to prevent merge conflicts
Close duplicates and already-fixed issues before writing any code
Audit issues (grep/search first) often need zero code changes
Stage only specific files with git add <file>, never git add -A
Investigate-first issues belong in the last wave, not the first
Always enable auto-merge immediately after gh pr create
Background agents lose task IDs — use foreground agents by default
applicable_to: Any project with GitHub and CI/CD
Codebases with good unit test coverage
